---
title: "Select Teachings from the Gospel of John"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    css: course-outline.css
    slide-number: true
    center: false
    margin: 0.05
---

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>Course Outline</h2>

<div class="lv-a"><a href="preamble/preamble.html">Preamble: Teaching Style and Philosophy</a></div>
<div class="lv-a"><a href="01-Intro/introduction.html">1. Introduction</a></div>
<div class="lv-a"><a href="02 - Jesus as God/jesus-as-god.html">2. Jesus as God</a> <span class="verse-ref">(1–3, 14)</span></div>
<div class="lv-a"><a href="03 - Jesus in Creation/jesus-in-creation.html">3. Jesus in Creation</a></div>
<div class="lv-a"><a href="04 - Jesus aa life and light/jesus-life-light.html">4. Jesus as Life and Light of Men</a></div>
<div class="lv-a"><a href="05 - Jesus in Salvation/jesus-in-salvation.html">5. Jesus in Salvation — New Birth</a></div>
<div class="lv-a"><a href="05 - Jesus grace truth and the law/grace-truth-law.html">6. Jesus: Grace and Truth vs. Moses and the Law</a></div>
<div class="lv-a"><a href="06 - Jesus and the Holy Spirit/holy-spirit.html">7. Jesus Baptizes with the Holy Spirit</a></div>
<div class="lv-a"><a href="07 - Jesus the Only way to the Father/only-way.html">8. Jesus: The Only Way to the Father</a></div>
<div class="lv-a"><a href="08 - Jesus priestly prayer/priestly-prayer.html">9. Jesus' Priestly Prayer</a></div>

</section>
```

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>Preamble: Teaching Style and Philosophy</h2>
<div class="lv-bullet">Encourage questions and discussion</div>
<div class="lv-bullet">Uses John as a starting point, but explores themes across all of Scripture</div>
<div class="lv-bullet">Prioritizes depth over coverage—no expectation to finish all material</div>
<div class="lv-bullet">Materials provided to registered participants</div>
</section>
```

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>I. Introduction</h2>

<details>
<summary><strong>A. Overview — Watch Video</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Bible Project — The Book of John</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <a href="https://bibleproject.com/guides/book-of-john/" target="_blank">bibleproject.com/guides/book-of-john/</a></div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>B. The Book</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Authorship and Date</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. <strong>Church Fathers</strong> (unanimous): John the Apostle, written in Ephesus, late 1st century</div>
<div class="lv-lc" style="margin-left:120px!important; font-size:0.66em!important;">— Irenaeus (c. 180 AD): "John, the disciple of the Lord… did himself publish a Gospel during his residence at Ephesus"</div>
<div class="lv-lc" style="margin-left:120px!important; font-size:0.66em!important;">— Clement of Alexandria (c. 215 AD): John wrote a "spiritual Gospel" after the Synoptics</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. <strong>Modern Scholarship</strong>: Anonymous; product of a Johannine community; c. 90–100 AD</div>
<div class="lv-lc" style="margin-left:120px!important; font-size:0.66em!important;">— The "Beloved Disciple" is the eyewitness source but the final author is debated</div>
<div class="lv-lc" style="margin-left:120px!important; font-size:0.66em!important;">— Earliest manuscript fragment (P52) dated c. 125–150 AD sets the upper limit</div>
<div class="lv-1" style="margin-top:0.4em!important;">2. Stated Purpose</div>
<div class="lv-lc-italic">a. John 20:31 — "…but these are written so that you may believe that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of God, and that by believing you may have life in his name."</div>
<div class="lv-1" style="margin-top:0.4em!important;">3. Distinctives of John</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. ~80–90% of content is unique — not found in Matthew, Mark, or Luke</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. Seven "I AM" Statements (John 6:35; 8:12; 10:7,9; 10:11,14; 11:25; 14:6; 15:1,5)</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. Seven Signs (John 2; 4; 5; 6:1–14; 6:16–21; 9; 11)</div>
<div class="lv-lc">d. Seven Titles of Jesus</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>C. About the Author</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Life of John</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. Born c. AD 6–15, Galilee — son of Zebedee and Salome</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. Called among first disciples; inner circle with Peter and James</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. The "Beloved Disciple" — remained at the crucifixion; entrusted with Mary's care</div>
<div class="lv-lc">d. Pillar of the Jerusalem church; founded churches across Asia Minor</div>
<div class="lv-lc">e. Exiled to Patmos under Domitian (c. AD 95) — wrote Revelation there</div>
<div class="lv-lc">f. Died naturally in Ephesus c. AD 100 — only apostle not martyred</div>
<div class="lv-1" style="margin-top:0.4em!important;">2. Christian Tradition</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. Attributed authorship: Gospel of John, 1–3 John, Revelation</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. Known for teaching: <em>"Little children, love one another"</em> (Jerome)</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. Disciple chain: John → Polycarp → Irenaeus — key line of apostolic tradition</div>
</details>

</section>
```

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>II. Jesus as God</h2>

<details>
<summary><strong>A. Seed of the Woman</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Old Testament Prophecy</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. Genesis 3:15 — "he shall bruise your head, and you shall bruise his heel"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. New Testament Fulfillments</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. Galatians 4:4 — "God sent forth his Son, born of woman…"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. Romans 16:20 — "The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. Hebrews 2:14 — "…through death he might destroy the one who has the power of death…"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">d. Revelation 12:9, 17 — the ancient serpent; war on her offspring</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>B. Seed of Abraham</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Old Testament Prophecy</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. Genesis 22:18 — "in your offspring shall all the nations of the earth be blessed"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. See also Genesis 12:3; 17:19</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. New Testament Fulfillments</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. Galatians 3:16 — Paul identifies Jesus as the singular seed</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. Galatians 3:29 — believers share the promise through union with Christ</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. Matthew 1:1 — "the son of David, the son of Abraham"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">d. Luke 3:34 — physical descent from Abraham affirmed</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>C. Old Testament Prophecies of a Divine Messiah</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Isaiah 9:6 — "Wonderful Counselor, Mighty God, Everlasting Father, Prince of Peace"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. Isaiah 7:14 — "the virgin shall conceive… Immanuel" (God with us)</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. Micah 5:2 — ruler from Bethlehem "whose origin is from of old, from ancient days"</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. Jeremiah 23:5–6 — "The Lord [YHWH] is our righteousness"</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>D. Melchizedek — Type of Christ's Eternal Priesthood</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Old Testament</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. Genesis 14:18–20 — king-priest receives tithes from Abraham</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. Psalm 110:4 — "You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. New Testament Application (Hebrews)</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. Hebrews 7:1–3 — "having neither beginning of days nor end of life…"</div>
<div class="lv-lc">b. Hebrews 7:15–17 — Jesus arises in the likeness of Melchizedek</div>
<div class="lv-lc">c. Hebrews 7:24–25 — "he holds his priesthood permanently… able to save to the uttermost"</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>E. The "Son of Man" — Divine Ruler (Daniel 7)</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Old Testament</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. Daniel 7:13–14 — given everlasting dominion over all peoples and nations</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. New Testament — Jesus Claims This Title</div>
<div class="lv-lc">a. Matthew 26:64 — "you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven"</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>F. Jesus' Seven "I AM" Statements in John</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. John 6:35 — "I am the bread of life"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. John 8:12 — "I am the light of the world"</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. John 10:9 — "I am the door"</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. John 10:11 — "I am the good shepherd"</div>
<div class="lv-1">5. John 11:25 — "I am the resurrection and the life"</div>
<div class="lv-1">6. John 14:6 — "I am the way, and the truth, and the life"</div>
<div class="lv-1">7. John 15:1 — "I am the true vine"</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>G. Gospel Accounts — People Declaring Jesus' Deity or Worshiping Him</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. John 20:28 — Thomas: "My Lord and my God!" (clearest verbal affirmation)</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. Matthew 16:16 — Peter: "You are the Christ, the Son of the living God"</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. Matthew 14:33 — Disciples worship after walking on water: "Truly you are the Son of God"</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. John 1:41 — Andrew: "We have found the Messiah"</div>
<div class="lv-1">5. Matthew 2:11 — The Magi fall down and worship the infant Jesus</div>
<div class="lv-1">6. Matthew 28:9 — Women at the resurrection take hold of his feet and worship him</div>
<div class="lv-1">7. Matthew 28:17 — The eleven disciples worship at the Great Commission</div>
<div class="lv-1">8. John 9:38 — Man born blind: "Lord, I believe." And he worshiped him</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>H. Gospel Accounts — People Declaring Jesus as the Christ / Messiah</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. John 1:41 — Andrew to Peter: "We have found the Messiah"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. Mark 8:29 — Peter at Caesarea Philippi: "You are the Christ"</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. Matthew 16:16 — Peter (fuller): "the Christ, the Son of the living God"</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. Luke 9:20 — Peter: "The Christ of God"</div>
<div class="lv-1">5. John 11:27 — Martha: "you are the Christ, the Son of God, who is coming into the world"</div>
<div class="lv-1">6. John 4:29, 42 — Samaritan woman and villagers: "the Savior of the world"</div>
<div class="lv-1">7. Matthew 14:33 — Disciples: "Truly you are the Son of God"</div>
<div class="lv-1">8. Mark 15:39 — Centurion at the cross: "Truly this man was the Son of God!"</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>I. Jesus' Pre-Existence Claims</strong> (primarily in John)</summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. John 8:58 — "before Abraham was, I am"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. John 17:5 — "the glory that I had with you before the world existed"</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. John 17:24 — "you loved me before the foundation of the world"</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. John 6:38 — "I have come down from heaven"</div>
<div class="lv-1">5. John 6:62 — "the Son of Man ascending to where he was before"</div>
<div class="lv-1">6. John 16:28 — "I came from the Father… going to the Father"</div>
<div class="lv-1">7. John 8:23 — "I am from above… I am not of this world"</div>
<div class="lv-1">8. John 3:13 — "he who descended from heaven, the Son of Man"</div>
</details>

<details>
<summary><strong>J. Additional New Testament Declarations of Jesus as God</strong></summary>
<div class="lv-1">1. Colossians 1:17 — "he is before all things, and in him all things hold together"</div>
<div class="lv-1">2. Colossians 2:9 — "the whole fullness of deity dwells bodily"</div>
<div class="lv-1">3. Philippians 2:5–6 — "in the form of God… equality with God"</div>
<div class="lv-1">4. Hebrews 1:8 — Father speaks of Son: "Your throne, O God, is forever and ever"</div>
<div class="lv-1">5. Romans 9:5 — "the Christ, who is God over all, blessed forever"</div>
<div class="lv-1">6. Titus 2:13 — "our great God and Savior Jesus Christ"</div>
<div class="lv-1">7. Philippians 2:10–11 — "every knee should bow… every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord" (Isaiah 45:23 applied to Jesus)</div>
</details>

</section>
```

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>III. Jesus in Creation</h2>
<div class="placeholder">(Content to be developed)</div>
</section>
```

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>IV. Jesus as Life and Light of Men</h2>
<div class="placeholder">(Content to be developed)</div>
</section>
```

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>V. Jesus in Salvation — New Birth</h2>
<div class="placeholder">(Content to be developed)</div>
</section>
```

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>VI. Jesus: Grace and Truth vs. Moses and the Law</h2>
<div class="placeholder">(Content to be developed)</div>
</section>
```

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>VII. Jesus Baptizes with the Holy Spirit</h2>
<div class="placeholder">(Content to be developed)</div>
</section>
```

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>VIII. Jesus: The Only Way to the Father</h2>
<div class="placeholder">(Content to be developed)</div>
</section>
```

```{=html}
<section class="slide level2">
<h2>IX. Jesus' Priestly Prayer</h2>
<div class="placeholder">(Content to be developed)</div>
</section>
```
